results.possibleIdentity: Peter Csanky is a Toronto-based sound engineer, designer, and musician active in the late 1960s and 1970s. He is noted for his technical contributions to the Canadian music scene, particularly in the development of innovative sound systems. Around 1968–1969, he designed the 'Csanky Sound System' for the band Lighthouse, a sophisticated and massive PA system that utilized custom-designed mixers with large tuner-style knobs rather than standard faders to handle the complex amplification needs of a large band featuring horns and strings. As a musician, Csanky joined the Toronto rock band Fludd in 1972 as a keyboardist, playing piano and mellotron on their second album, '...On!', and their hit single 'Cousin Mary.' He was a pioneer in the use of the mellotron in Canada, reportedly being one of the first to import the instrument from the UK and owning several models. His technical expertise extended to the physical design of equipment and lighting. In the 1980s, he was recorded as a licensed amateur radio operator in Toronto with the call sign VE3IKX. His work as an engineer collaborating with artists aligns with the mission of the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization.
